description: January 27, 2001, April 26, 2001, July 28, 2001: Minutes of presbytery meetings have not been approved. RAO 14-3.c.8
Ordination exam approved without record of internship being completed. BCO 21-2; 21-4
A call approved for labor Out of Bounds gives no reasons for considering it a valid Christian ministry. BCO 20-1
Presbytery meeting held without minutes being submitted. BCO 13-11
No record of committee members, licentiates or interns in Directory or Roll. RAO 14-4.C.1 d. That the following responses to the 28th GA
There is no documentation (calls, etc.) for two men for changes from assistant to associate pastor. There are no commissions approved for installation. BCO 15-1; 15-2.
responses: These men were already
ordained and installed in their particular churches.
This was simply a change in their title designation.
Change in call from Assistant to Associate
requires a new call from the congregation to replace
previous call from the session. BCO 20-6, 22-2,3
These two men in question
were serving in their churches and still are.
Congregational Meetings were called and calls were
extended to them as Associate Pastors. There were no
changes in the terms of their calls at that time.
Calvary Presbytery felt that since these men were
already ordained and installed, that a further
installation service would not be necessary. If we
were wrong in this we will gladly correct the situation,
if you will so instruct us. We thought we did
everything correctly, but we willingly submit to your
wisdom and humbly seek your guidance in this matter
if further action is needed.
Rationale why response to 29th GA is

description: Incomplete record of exam elements. BCO 21-4b,c,d; RAO 14-3.e.5 d. That the following responses to the 29th GA
No record of source of request to dissolve relationship with an Assistant Pastor. BCO 23-1
No record of Presbytery approving call to TE. BCO 20-1
responses: This was indeed a serious oversight on our
part, and Presbytery has rectified the situation by
approving the following motion at our stated meeting
October 27, 2001 to dissolve the relationship between
TE [name deleted] with Redeemer PCA, Winston
Salem, NC as Associate Pastor (minutes of this
congregational meeting are attached as Appendix #6).
Also, at its October 27, 2001 meeting CCP approved
the call of TE [name deleted] as Assistant Pastor of
Grace Church, Kemersville. We believe what
happened is that all the appropriate transfer steps were
not recorded because Presbytery viewed this as an in-
house transfer. We will be more careful in the future.
Adopted

description: Incomplete record of exam elements. BCO 21-4; RAO 14-3.e.5
No record of judicial commission executive session. RAO 14-3-e.6
Business conducted at Called Meeting not included in the stated purpose of meeting. BCO 13-12.
No record of congregational meeting requesting dissolution of pastoral relation. BCO 23-1
No record of call included in minutes after approved ordination exam. BCO 20-1; 21-1 d. That the following responses to the 29th GA
No indication that a congregational meeting was held to approve dissolution of pastoral relationship. BCO 23-1.
responses: I have spoken to the Chairman of the
Ministers Committee who is responsible for the action
taken concerning the dissolution of pastoral
relationship. There was a congregational meeting
which was appropriately called and conducted. The
Secretary of the committee failed to include the
minutes of that meeting in the minutes of the
committee meeting. The Chairman of this committee
will ensure that from this point on all minutes of
Congregational Meetings which dissolve the pastoral
relationship will be recorded in their minutes and
reported to Presbytery.

description: Item 19.C & 19.D: Teaching Elders were approved to labor out of bounds without following BCO 8-7 and 13-2.
responses: We acknowledge that we failed to follow
the BCO 8-7 and 13-2 in approving Teaching Elders to
labor outside the bounds of Presbytery. Since being
made aware of our error we will be careful to follow the
proper procedures as outlined in BCO 8-7 and 13-2.
Adopted

description: No record of congregational meetings to dissolve pastoral relationship. BCO 23-1.
responses: The congregational meeting to concur with
Mr. [name deletedj’s resignation was held January
2000 and concurred. The fact was not reported in
Presbytery’s meeting. Presbytery will seek to be more
diligent to include such details in our minutes in the
future.

description: Minister examined for transfer with no record of previous ecclesiastical connection. BCO 13-6.
responses: Presbytery failed to request transfer of
credentials in a timely manner. The Stated Clerk
spoke by phone to the Stated Clerk of the dismissing
Presbytery and sent a written request for the transfer.
Presbytery failed to enter these in the minutes.
Presbytery will seek to be more diligent in the future
to do these things in a timely manner and to enter them
in the minutes. Adopted

description: no record of terms of call. BCO 21-4; RAO 14-3e.5 Incomplete record of exam elements. RAO 14-3e.5; BCO 21-4 d. That the following responses to the 29th GA
Call for meeting is not in order. BCO 13-12 requires 3RE’s & 3TE’s from 3 churches.
responses: Great Lakes Presbytery, as well as the
former Stated Clerk of GLP, acknowledge their error
in listing only 2 Teaching Elders and 2 Ruling Elders
representing 3 churches in the call for the meeting, and
will be more careful in the future to document the
concurrence of at least 3 Teaching Elders and 3 Ruling
Elders from 3 churches as required in BCO 13-12.

description: No indication that candidate for licensure preached a sermon as part of his exam. BCO 19-2.
responses: Great Lakes Presbytery verifies that the
candidate did, indeed preach a sermon at that meeting
and that the sermon was approved by Presbytery. We
regret that the itemizing of the individual requirements
for licensure was not consistent with the action of
Presbytery recorded as ‘On separate motions all areas
of his exam were sustained’, and will be more careful
to be consistent in the future. Adopted

description: Presbytery approved an exception to WCF 24-3 in which the candidate stated, “the WCF has gone too far in the section on marriage where in Chapter 24-3, it lumps Roman Catholics, infidels, and other idolaters together,” allowing him to “express his views” without clarifying what “express his views” means and whether he may practice his views. BCO 26-1; 24-5

Presbytery approved two candidates with exceptions to WCF 24-3 in which they stated, “the WCF has gone too far in the section on marriage where in Chapter 24-3, it lumps Roman Catholics, infidels, and other idolaters together.” BCO 26-1; 24-5

August 18-19, 2000, November 10-11, 2000: No records of commission reports. BCO 15- 1; 13-11.
responses: Heartland Presbytery agrees with the
exception and apologizes for failing to include these
commission reports in its minutes. As is evident from
the minutes, the clerk intended to insert them, but forgot
to do so. We have attempted to locate these reports for
your review, but have only been able to find one: the
Report of the Commission to Install [name deleted] on
August 20, 2000. We will submit this report for review
by the 30th General Assembly CRPR. The other reports
were purged with the assumption that they had been
included in the minutes. We will be more careful in this
area in the future. Adopted

description: Man approved for ordination who takes the following exceptions (among several others) to certain language in the Confession of Faith: does not agree that “vows of poverty, obedience, and singleness are necessarily sinful and superstitious” WCF XXII. 7; takes exception to the language that “none who are not then present in the congregation” should partake of the Lord’s Supper WCF XXIX.3; takes exception to the language of “the making any representation of God, of all or any of the three persons, either inwardly in our mind or outwardly in any kind of image or likeness of any creature whatsoever” WLC #109; takes exception to the language “Although all are not to be permitted to read the word publicly to the congregation...” WLC #156. BCO 21-5, q. 2; BCO 26-1.
Extraordinary clause invoked without record of reasons, record of super majority vote, or commission to install. SCO 21-4 d. That the following responses to the 29th GA
Commissions were erected to install TEs, but there are no subsequent reports in or attached to the minutes. BCO 15-1.
responses: Presbytery regrets not having approved
and appended the minutes of the Commissions to
install TE [name deleted] and TE [name deleted] in
their November 10, 2000 meeting minutes. The report
of the Commission for TE [name deleted] was
approved and appended in our January 20, 2001
meeting minutes and likewise the Commission to
install TE [name deleted] in our March 9, 2001
meeting minutes. The records for the Commission to
install TE [name deleted] seem to have been lost. He
was however (re)installed on November 4, 2001 and
that Commission report was approved and appended in
our November 9, 2001 meeting minutes.

description: A TE, transferring from another denomination was not examined as required by BCO 21-4, nor were there extraordinary provisions invoked, nor was he asked the ordination questions. BCO 13-6.
responses: Presbytery now understand its BCO 13-6
requirement to examine ministers transferring from
another denomination in both views and knowledge in
all areas of BCO 21-4 and has started that process with
its January 12, 2002 meeting when it examined a
minister coming from the KAPC. Presbytery also
understands its requirement to orally ask the ordination
questions of BCO 21-5 at the examination. We also
note that we accomplished this in written form when
the transferring minister signed the Ministerial
Obligation Form. Presbytery notes that our minutes of
June 8, 2001 (the examination of Rev. [name deleted]
for transfer from the Free Methodist Church) will fail
to conform to the above standards and requests the
2002 Committee on Review of Presbytery Minutes to
note our current resolve to meet our BCO
requirements. (This June 8, 2001 examination was
conducted before the 2001 Committee drew our
attention to this expanded requirement)

description: Approval of the examination for ordination of a candidate who states as part of his exceptions to the WCF, WLC, and WSC that “I also believe the Sabbath rest to mean any one day in seven.” WCF 21-7; WLC 116; WSC 59
responses: In the examination of Candidate [name
deleted] for ordination, Presbytery regrets allowing the
candidate’s own statement “I believe the Sabbath rest
to mean any one day in seven,” to appear in our
minutes with all its ambiguity. We understand how
that statement might be interpreted as the view that we
might choose another day of the week besides Sunday
as the day for corporate worship. The Committee’s
references to WCF 21-7 and to WLC 116 imply that
they so interpreted [name deleted]’s statement. But
that is not his view. His intention is to affirm that
ministers, unable to rest on the Lord’s Day due to their
official duties, are to choose another day of the week
on which to rest. Presbytery does not consider this
view an unacceptable exception to our Standards and
trusts the Committee agrees. Adopted

description: Incomplete exams. RAO 14-3e.5; BCO 21-4
Incomplete record of TE transfer exam elements. BCO 13-6; RAO 14- 3.e.5
Minutes submitted incomplete. BCO 13-11; RAO 14-1 d. That the responses to the 29th GA exceptions be

Man licensed who “holds exception in his view of the Sabbath (he does not believe that the Sabbath Day and Lord’s Day are the same), the law (he is not absolutely sure that the Decalogue is the New Testament Ethic), and on who may administer the Sacraments (he cannot see that the administration of the sacraments is the sole prerogative of teaching elders; he believes that ruling elders and others who have demonstrated knowledge, understanding, and maturity concerning the sacraments should be allowed to administer them.)” (WCF 21-7, 27-4, 19-2).
responses: The Presbytery has submitted word-for-
word the same response submitted to the 29th GA and
the same response in substance was submitted to the
27th and 28th General Assemblies. Therefore, the 30th
GA believes that the Presbytery still needs to submit a
substantive response to the action of these previous
General Assemblies.
See 29th GA Minutes

description: Items 75- 17.f., 75-17.g., and 75-17.i, refer to appendices which are not attached.
responses: None submitted. Adopted

Incomplete record of exam elements. BCO 21-4; RAO 14-3.e.5 d. That the following responses to the 29th GA
Candidates are consistently received with no record that the provisions of BCO 18- 2 were met.
responses: Presbytery regrets the failure to record
meeting the provision of BCO 18-2 for Candidates
coming under care, and will endeavor to be more
inclusive in the future.

description: April 18, 2000, August 29, 2000,
p.4, #1,3; No evidence of congregational concurrence in dissolution of calls. BCO 23-1.
responses: Presbytery regrets the failure to record the
congregational concurrence to the dissolution of
pastoral relationship in items 1 and 3. The
congregational concurrence was received in each case
but not recorded.

description: p.8 , #4,5; Two commissions are established with only one ruling elder member.
responses: Presbytery regrets the failure to record the
proper number of Ruling Elders appointed to
commissions. In each case the commissions were
compliant with the BCO but the names were not
recorded. Presbytery will take measures to ensure
proper recording in the future.

description: p.5, A candidate is brought under care when he is not present so he could not answer the question or receive a charge. Also, there is no record of a charge to or prayer fora candidate who is present. BCO 18-3.
responses: Presbytery regrets the failure to properly
record all of the details concerning examination and
charge. In the future all candidates will be present and
all elements will be recorded, as required.

description: p.5, A candidate is approved for ordination with no record in the minutes of a call or of provisions for ordination. BCO 20-1; 21-5.
responses: Presbytery regrets the failure to include
the candidate’s call and commission and will endeavor
to be more careful in the future. Adopted

description: There is no reason given or documentation for removal of a candidate from Under Care Roll. BCO 18-7.
responses: The two candidates were removed because
both were ordained.

description: Divesting of TE should report 2nd reading. BCO 38-2. Also should assign to church membership. BCO 46-8.
responses: The action taken was the second reading
(see previous meeting Minutes of the 60th SM of NTP,
Page 5, ppg 2101). A letter was received 6 months
earlier from the TE wherein he requested divestiture.
It was reported to the Presbytery and he was divested
at the next meeting. This was not unilateral but based
on a request. Our action was in accordance with BCO
38-2. Regarding the exception to BCO 46-8, the TE
requesting divestiture had moved to an adjoining state
and a town without a PCA church. The TE indicated
that he would be attending a different denomination.

description: Examination of TE for transfer from another denomination: He was not examined in Bible content, church history or history of the PCA. BCO 13-6; 21-4.
responses: The candidate was examined in the
previously mentioned fields but the SC failed to record
same. The transferring TE was formerly the
Moderator of the National Presbyterian Church of
Mexico and was examined thoroughly in Committee.
However, the point is moot since the TE has been
transferred back to Mexico and is no longer a member
of NTP or the PCA. In the future, the SC will attempt
to record examination action more thoroughly and
completely.

description: No record that man was a member of a church of nt p for 6 months or that he had the endorsement of his session. BCO 20-1; 13-10.
responses: The Stated Clerk inadvertently omitted
this information. Letters and records in the files
indicate them an was a member of ant p church for 6
months and the files do contain a letter of sessional
endorsement (dated 6/11/2000). The SC will take
greater pains not to leave this detail out of the Minutes
in the future. Adopted

paragraph 31.10, concerning the ordination examination of a man. BCO 21-4. There is no indication of completion of internship. There was no sermon approved and no documentation of his call. ashe was coming as a Licentiate, there was no indication of his being asked if he changed his previous views concerning the WCF, WSC, or the BCO. BCO 21-4.
responses: Southeast Louisiana Presbytery agrees
with the exceptions, except for the preaching portion.
We will make every attempt to be more careful in
reporting all actions taken by Presbytery. All actions
cited in the exception were accomplished but not
recorded.
In reference to a sermon not being preached by one
who is licensed, the examination for licensure requires
a sermon to be preached and BCO 21-4 par. #2
specifically states that “If the Presbytery previously
approved all parts of the licensure examination, it need
not re-examine the intern in those areas at this time.”

description: In view of the candidate’s exception to WCF 11:3, as stated below, which strikes at the fundamentals of our system of doctrine, the Presbytery of Southern Florida erred in sustaining his examination for transfer. With regard to WCF 11:3, “I do not believe that the Holy Spirit eternally proceeds from the Father and the Son, but, in the integrity of the Holy Trinity, from the Father alone. I believe that the references such as John 14:16-17, 26, 15:26, 16:7-15, refer to the out-pouring of the Spirit at Pentecost and not to His eternal procession. While it is true, of course, that the Holy Spirit is the Spirit of Christ, he is also referred to as the Spirit of God, and, as far as that goes, in the mystery of the Holy Trinity, Jesus is referred to as the Everlasting Father. Historically, the addition of the filioque was made unilaterally by the Roman Catholic Church centuries after the Council of Nicea and subsequently inherited by Protestantism.” d. The response to the July 20, 1999 and October 19, 1999 exceptions of the 28th GA be found
BCO 13-6 requires thorough exam of transfers in all areas. No extraordinary clause stated as rationale.
responses: Presbytery has discontinued using D-21,
Check List For Reception of a Minister From Another
Denomination, which is part of the “Handbook for
Presbytery Clerks.” Presbytery is now following BCO
13-6 in all such transfers.

description: BCO 13-6 requires thorough exam of transfers in all areas. No extraordinary clause found.

description: Theological paper approved in which candidate stated, “I believe that God gives extraordinary gifts (miracles, healing, and tongues).” WCF 18-3; WLC #80; BCO 7-1.
responses: The Teaching Elder in question is no
longer a member of Presbytery and has left the PCA
and joined another denomination.
RAO 14-10.b requires that the Presbytery
respond by stating whether they agree or respectfully
disagree with the exception. Adopted

description: Incomplete record of exam elements. BCO 21-4; RAO 14-3.e.5
Incomplete record of exam elements. BCO 19-2c; RAO 14-3.e.5 d. The following responses to the 29th GA exceptions
The purpose of the called meeting is not stated with the minutes. BCO 13-12.
responses: The purpose of this Called Meeting was
specified in the Call of May 17, 2000. A copy of which
is attached [with the minutes turned in this year]. There
were only two items of business to be conducted:
(1) Approve the Pastoral Call from Covenant
Presbyterian Church, Sun City West, AZ, to TE [name
deleted], (2) Conduct appropriate examination of
[name deleted] as the RUF Representative at the
University of Arizona and approve his moving to the
field.
As you will note from paragraphs 1 and 2 of
the June 30, 2000 minutes, these were the only two
items discussed and acted on. BCO 13-12 does not say
that the purpose of the Called Meeting has to be
explicitly restated in the minutes if it has been clearly
stated in the Call which in this case it was.
The moderator, RE [name deleted], explicitly
ruled that we would follow the order as the business
items were listed on the docket. This was done as
paragraphs 1 & 2 so indicate. It appears, therefore,
that the Southwest Presbytery fully complied with
BCO 13-12. However, we have retroactively changed
out June 30, 2000 minutes to specifically reflect the
two items of business mentioned in the May 17, 2000
Call. Adopted

responses: It was moved/seconded/passed to
communicate to the General Assembly that the Presbytery
of Southwest Florida recognizes and regrets its mistake
and will seek to be more diligent in the future. Adopted
